twitter_thread: 47% of private compartments were empty on my 12hr Krakow sleeper train. Overbooked or underpriced?
Saved €40 by checking prices on 3 websites: PolishTrains, RailEurope, and Trainline
Double-check the train station: Krakow's sleeper train departs from Krakow Główny, not Krakow Płaszów
Packing tip: 1 carry-on bag only, as luggage racks are non-existent on the Krakow sleeper
Quiet car not enforced: 23 noise complaints on my 12hr journey. Bring earplugs
Avoid 6am arrival: only 5 taxis available at Krakow station, with 200+ passengers waiting
Have you taken the Krakow sleeper train? What was your experience like? #krakowtravel #sleepertrain
pack.linkedin: I took the Krakow sleeper train and found 47% of private compartments empty, prompting me to investigate the pricing strategy. By comparing prices on PolishTrains, RailEurope, and Trainline, I saved €40 on my ticket. To avoid last-minute issues, it's crucial to verify the train station and schedule. The train has a quiet car, but with 23 noise complaints on my journey, earplugs are essential. What are your experiences with sleeper trains, and do you have tips for a smoother journey?
tiktok_hooks: I spent 12 hours on a train to Krakow and found almost half the private compartments empty
Checked 3 websites, saved €40 on my Krakow sleeper train ticket
Just took the Krakow sleeper train and I'm sharing my top tips
pack.reddit_headline: Krakow sleeper train: 47% empty private compartments, is this a pricing strategy fail?
